mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tightening labor market conditions that boost aggregate wage income and corporate profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, the rank correlation (IC) between 3-month UNRATE change and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (i.e., 95% CI of IC includes 0), or the Sharpe ratio of a long-short portfolio based on UNRATE signals is not greater than 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeding 25 basis points signals a deliberate monetary tightening by the Fed that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following FEDFUNDS 3-month increase > 0.25% is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate (fraction of negative returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) and industrial production (INDPRO) is growing at a positive month-over-month rate, the combination of cheap financing and expanding output creates a strong economic tailwind that lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year walk-forward, the average 20-day return when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and INDPRO growth > 0% is not significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term in a linear regression is not significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, a sequential two-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any rolling 2-year period within the post-GFC recovery, the rank IC of HOUST 2-month change vs 60-day forward returns is not positive, or the average return in decile with highest HOUST growth is not higher than lowest decile by at least 200 bps annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer spending momentum, which is a primary driver of corporate revenues and earnings; this signal consistently predicts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The cross-sectional rank IC of RSAFS 1-month change with 20-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 1-year walk-forward window, or the long-short portfolio returns have a Sharpe ratio below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below the year-over-year change in the CPI (CPIAUCSL), real interest rates are negative, creating a monetary environment that encourages leverage and risk-taking in equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL 12m growth, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided p > 0.05), or the strategy fails to produce positive returns in more than 60% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 6-month growth rate in industrial production (INDPRO) signals an imminent contraction in manufacturing activity, which typically precedes declines in aggregate corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return after INDPRO 6-month growth turns negative is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces more false positives than true negatives (hit rate < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) below 100,000 jobs indicates a sudden weakening in labor demand that erodes consumer confidence and earnings expectations, causing a negative drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a PAYEMS monthly change below 100,000 is not negative at the 5% significance level, or the signal does not predict returns better than a random classifier (AUC < 0.55).
